content:
- Finalized task 28 completion workflow: updated task markdown status/checkboxes/passing tags, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed task changes + evidence + AGENTS learning, and pushed commit `8c73f97` to `master`.
- Required gates were rerun after clippy-driven code edits and all passed: make check, make test, make test-bdd, make lint.
- Done; ready to quit immediately unless a new task is assigned.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md end-to-end after loading progress log.
- Applied selection rules strictly: all bug tasks are passing; there are non-bug tasks with <passes>false</passes> so meta-task did not take precedence.
- Picked next logical unblocked failing task as story-rust-system-harness task 29 (TLS adversarial e2e hardening) and wrote its path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- Next agent should execute task 29 implementation and validation.

- Started by reading prior memory (`.ralph/progress_read.sh`) and loaded active task from `.ralph/current_task.txt`: `29-task-e2e-tls-adversarial-cert-validation.md`.
- Inspected task state and detected planning phase (no lifecycle marker yet), then ran parallel discovery across TLS-related files and tests to map current coverage and gaps.
- Verified that `src/api/worker.rs` already contains substantial TLS adversarial tests (wrong CA, hostname mismatch, expired cert, mTLS trusted/untrusted paths), and identified likely gap around reusable harness-level adversarial fixture APIs and broader black-box integration clarity.
- Added a full detailed execution plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/29-task-e2e-tls-adversarial-cert-validation.md` with module-by-module changes, phased execution, verification commands, and explicit skeptical verification requirement.
- Appended `TO BE VERIFIED` at end of task file per workflow requirement.
- Next run should perform deep skeptical verification (alter at least one concrete plan item), document the delta, then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE`.

- Loaded current task 29 in TO BE VERIFIED state, ran a deep skeptical verification pass with broad parallel inspections, and revised the task plan with concrete deltas (removed speculative cluster TLS scope, mandated shared fixture extraction, added explicit marker checks).
- Executed plan: added shared `#[cfg(test)]` TLS cert/config/adversarial fixture builders in `src/test_harness/tls.rs`, added fixture unit tests, and refactored `src/api/worker.rs` TLS security tests to consume those helpers while deleting duplicated rcgen/rustls helper code.
- Ran targeted tests (`cargo test --lib test_harness::tls`, `cargo test --lib api::worker::tests::security`) and full gates (`make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`) with deterministic env vars and captured logs under `.ralph/evidence/story-rust-system-harness/29-task-e2e-tls-adversarial-cert-validation/gates/`.
- Marker grep results: `evaluation failed` absent in make test/lint logs; `congratulations` marker not emitted by current gate outputs. Recorded this explicitly in task evidence.
- Updated task file status/checklists to done with `<passing>true</passing>` and appended a new AGENTS learning about keeping rcgen-powered helpers behind `#[cfg(test)]`.
- Next immediate steps: run task switch script, stage relevant task/code/evidence/progress files, commit with required message format including gate evidence, and push.
